preamble: The substitution of the voiceless dental fricative [θ] with the voiceless bilabial stop [p] is a form of labialization and stopping, primarily observed in child language acquisition and specific creole varieties.

examples.note: Common substitution in early language development where the complex dental fricative is replaced by a simpler bilabial stop.
Substitution of the dental fricative with a bilabial stop.
Occurs in some varieties where speakers over-compensate for the labial nature of the following rhotic or substitute peripheral consonants.
